events.title: Moose on the Loose Kid #4
events.description: Sept 12 - Utah Olympic Park - Moose on the Loose Race Finale Join us for Moose on the Loose 2026, a four-race trail running series in Park City for kids ages 3–14. Built to be fun, welcoming, and confidence-building, the series gives young runners a chance to race on local trails, stay active, and enjoy a supportive community atmosphere. Recommended race distances are available by age to help athletes find the right fit. The 2026 race dates are June 13, July 18, August 15, and September 12, with all races beginning at 9:00 AM. This year’s race venues include RTS Trailhead, Round Valley North, Quinn's Trailhead, and Utah Olympic Park, offering a variety of exciting trail settings throughout the summer. Athletes may register for individual races at $25 each or sign up for the full four-race series and save $5 per race, for a total savings of $20....

events.title: Women's Epic 10K at Deer Valley
events.description: Women's Epic Race at Deer Valley Resort is a 10k loop with a 1335ft vertical trek to the peak and back down. You can hike, jog, or run your journey through gorgeous trees with changing fall leaves and steep climbs. You will undoubtedly come off the mountain with more confidence and grit after completing this race to the top of the world-class Deer Valley Resort located in Park City Utah. And when you get to the finish line you can grab a drink and celebrate!

events.title: Live PC Give PC
events.description: Live PC Give PC will take place on FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 6, 2026! HELP SHAPE PARK CITY’S FUTURE IN A SINGLE DAY. Every November, Live PC Give PC brings our community together through LivePCGivePC.org for 24 hours of extreme generosity. Children break open their piggy banks. Young people dip into their savings. Businesses kick in matching grants. With everyone in the community giving whatever they can, we’ve raised millions of dollars for local nonprofits. It’s how we’re working together to preserve the place we love—so it will continue to thrive for generations to come.
events.location: Park City, UT

events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

events.description: In one class you will learn the elements of a striking photograph and how to create one. Learn the basics of composing dramatic photos and become familiar with fun iPhone camera features and editing tools. events.title: Circular Garden
events.description: *Circular Garden* brings together botanical paintings by Carol O'Malia and abstract pointillist works by AJ Oishi in a vibrant exploration of nature, color, and inner transformation. In her newest series, O’Malia reinterprets the lush floral compositions of Rachel Ruysch, the celebrated 17th- and 18th-century Dutch painter known for her exquisite still lifes. While honoring Ruysch’s extraordinary detail and layered arrangements, O’Malia introduces a contemporary sensibility through luminous natural backgrounds and subtle abstract elements that breathe new life into the tradition of botanical painting. Oishi’s serene, meditative paintings are composed of thousands of carefully placed dots and harmonious complementary colors. The recurring circular forms throughout her work symbolize individuality, growth, and the ongoing journey toward a more mindful and balanced life....
events.location: Julie Nester Gallery, Park City, UT

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: a sound bath journey
events.description: a sound bath journey Tuesday, July 21st | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required Step away from the busyness of life and into a space of deep rest, relaxation, and renewal. Surrounded by the beautiful Wasatch mountains, this immersive sound bath experience is designed to help calm the mind, regulate the nervous system, and support the body's natural ability to heal. Through the soothing vibrations of sound, you'll be invited to unwind, soften, and simply receive. Allow yourself to be held as you relax into a state of ease and surrender. No experience is necessary-just come as you are! Please bring anything that will help you feel comfortable and supported during this deeply restorative experience: a yoga mat, blanket, pillow, eye mask, water bottle, or any other cozy items you might need. Mary is a 500-hour Certified Yoga Instructor and Trauma-Informed Breathwork Facilitator-in-Training based in Heber City, Utah. Passionate about nervous system regulation, she uses sound as a powerful tool to help calm the mind, restore balance, and create space for deep relaxation and healing. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Mozart’s “Jupiter”
events.description: Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner leads an all-Mozart program starring Concertmaster Madeline Adkins in Mozart’s second violin concerto. Written when he was just 18, the concerto showcases effortless virtuosity with the elegance and clarity that would continue to define his voice throughout his career. Framing the concerto are Mozart’s two final symphonies—written just weeks apart—revealing the fascinating journey of the composer's musical maturation and his unmistakable brilliance. One of Mozart’s most recognizable works, his Symphony No. 40 uses a minor key—a rare choice for the composer—to build emotional intensity and dark lyricism. In contrast, the program concludes with the joyful Symphony No. 41, whose grandeur and musical tour de force ultimately earned it the nickname “Jupiter,” honoring the Roman god....

events.title: Wildflower Wine Dinner with Terrestoria
events.description: Celebrate the height of wildflower season in Alta with **Snowpine Lodge’s Wildflower Wine Dinner**, an open-air culinary experience surrounded by 360-degree alpine views and summer blooms. Enjoy a four-course menu, thoughtfully paired with wines from Utah’s own **Terrestoria** for an immersive journey through Italy’s diverse wine landscape. The evening’s menu features Roasted Beet Carpaccio, Ravioli Florentine, Elk Loin with juniper jus, and Dark Chocolate Crémeux, each paired with selections from regions including Valle d'Itria, Friuli Colli Orientali, and Basilicata. Skip the drive home and extend your getaway with one or more nights at Snowpine Lodge. Exclusive rates for Wildflower Wine Dinner guests start at **$135 per night** (available while supplies last). Simply purchase your wine dinner ticket, then email **reservations@snowpine.com** to book your stay and receive the special rate....

events.title: Circular Garden
events.description: *Circular Garden* brings together botanical paintings by Carol O'Malia and abstract pointillist works by AJ Oishi in a vibrant exploration of nature, color, and inner transformation. In her newest series, O’Malia reinterprets the lush floral compositions of Rachel Ruysch, the celebrated 17th- and 18th-century Dutch painter known for her exquisite still lifes. While honoring Ruysch’s extraordinary detail and layered arrangements, O’Malia introduces a contemporary sensibility through luminous natural backgrounds and subtle abstract elements that breathe new life into the tradition of botanical painting. Oishi’s serene, meditative paintings are composed of thousands of carefully placed dots and harmonious complementary colors. The recurring circular forms throughout her work symbolize individuality, growth, and the ongoing journey toward a more mindful and balanced life....

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....

events.title: 38 Special
events.description: 38 Special – Live in Concert Bring on the anthems. 38 Special delivers high-energy, Southern-fried rock that defined an era and still packs the house decades later. Known for blending catchy hooks with driving guitar riffs, the band has become a staple of American rock radio and live performance. With iconic hits like “Hold On Loosely,” “Caught Up in You,” and “Rockin’ Into the Night,” 38 Special has built a legacy of feel-good, sing-along songs that cross generations. Their live shows are tight, powerful, and packed with the kind of rock-and-roll energy that turns a concert into a shared memory. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this is a show made for fans of classic rock, summer nights, and timeless music that still hits just right. Fox Brothers Band Get ready for an unforgettable night of high-energy entertainment with the Fox Brothers Band, one of Utah’s most sought-after live music acts. Blending the best of Country, Rock, and Pop, the band delivers a dynamic performance packed with crowd favorites, modern hits, and timeless classics that keep audiences singing and dancing all night long. Known for their infectious energy, exceptional musicianship, and engaging stage presence, the Fox Brothers Band has built a reputation for creating memorable live experiences for audiences of all ages. The band's members have individually performed with or opened for nationally recognized artists including Chris Janson, Riley Green, Chase Rice, Ian Munsick, Brantley Gilbert, Neon Trees, and many others.

While honoring Ruysch’s extraordinary detail and layered arrangements, O’Malia introduces a contemporary sensibility through luminous natural backgrounds and subtle abstract elements that breathe new life into the tradition of botanical painting. Oishi’s serene, meditative paintings are composed of thousands of carefully placed dots and harmonious complementary colors. The recurring circular forms throughout her work symbolize individuality, growth, and the ongoing journey toward a more mindful and balanced life....
events.location: Julie Nester Gallery, Park City, UT

events.description: Step into an open-air culinary celebration set against the sweeping Wasatch Mountains. Taste of Montage brings together signature dishes from Montage Deer Valley’s dining destinations, including Apex, Yama Sushi, Daly’s Pub & Rec and Après Lounge, alongside interactive local pop-ups, seasonal pastries and thoughtfully paired libations. Live music and mountain ambiance create a vibrant yet refined setting, inviting guests to explore each tasting at a relaxed pace. The evening unfolds as a curated progression of flavor and place, celebrating the creativity and spirit of the resort’s culinary team....
events.location: Montage Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Speedgoat Mountain Races by UTMB®
events.description: Embark on the adventure of a lifetime at the Speedgoat Mountain Races by UTMB®. Take on the trails winding throughout Snowbird in the 10k, 28k or 50k. Dig deep to unleash your inner grit and be rewarded with breathtaking panoramic views of Little Cottonwood Canyon. While here, enjoy the amenities of a world-renowned ski resort throughout race weekend, including lodging, a variety of dining options and post-race relaxation at The Cliff Spa. All races start and finish on the Plaza Deck at the Snowbird Center, Entry 2. The race village is located in the Upper Circle parking lot outside the Snowbird Center. Limited Tram rides will be offered for spectators on the morning of each race day. Lodging discounts are available for registered participants. Parking is available for participants, friends, family and spectators in Snowbird's main lots. Race Details: 10k \(6.2 Miles\) Date: Friday, July 24 Start Time: 9 am Elevation Gain: 1,640\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center 30k \(19.2 Miles\) Date: Friday, July 24 Start Time: 7:30 am Elevation Gain: 6,561\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center 50k \(31 Miles\) Date: Saturday, July 25 Start Time: 6:30 am Elevation Gain: 11,318\+ ft Start/Finish: The Plaza Deck at The Snowbird Center...

events.title: Main Street Walking Tour
events.description: Put on your walking shoes and explore Park City’s Historic Main Street! Explore the architecture and stories of Park City’s unique history-from the days as a booming mine town to an emerging ski town. The Park City Museum’s popular Historic Main Street Public Walking Tours are Monday through Friday at 2 p.m. from June 29 to September 19. Meet at the Museum store. Cost is $15 per person. No tours on August 8 \(Kimball Arts Festival\) and September 7 \(Labor Day/Miners’ Day\). The tour is most appropriate for ages 13 and up. Please arrive 10 minutes early, wear comfortable walking shoes, bring water, and wear sun protection. The tour lasts about one hour and fifteen minutes. If you have general questions about the tour or want to schedule a private tour for twelve or more people, please contact Diane at 435-574-9554....
events.location: Park City Museum, Park City, UT

events.title: The Last Light
events.description: Join us for an incredible night of good vibes and heavy grooves as we bring our unique style of high desert reggae, rock, and groove music to Park City! The Last Light is a reggae based groove collective composed of 7 artists who call the Southern Utah Desert home. With a lineup of players each with their own deep musical resumes and variety of styles to bring to the table, they've set out to create a brand new sound blending reggae, rock, hip-hop, & funk together into an electric sound. They Released their Debut EP, "Music Is The Medicine" in 2023 and have performed shows in Utah, Nevada, California, & Florida in the last 4 years. Their newest single “I’ll Be Ok” was released 2/27/26. Always with the crowd in mind, they bring all of their energy to their performances with one singular goal - to get you on your feet, out of your mind, and into the music. Bringing full spectrum vibes that mirror a complete human experience - The Last Light's three vocalists, Charlie, Kaleb, and Eli will bring you lyrics that make you feel, lyrics that make you laugh, and together they bring thick grooves that make you dance....
events.location: The Basin Recreation Fieldhouse, Park City, UT

events.title: Taylor Dayne
events.description: Taylor Dayne Taylor Dayne is an American pop icon. With a career spanning three decades, Taylor’s Groundbreaking debut single “Tell It To My Heart” turned her into an overnight international star in 1987. She followed the smash hit with seventeen Top 20 singles over the course of her three-decade career, including Number Ones “Love Will Lead You Back”, “Prove Your Love”, and “I’ll Always Love You.” Taylor has sold over 75 million albums and singles worldwide, earned three GRAMMY nominations, an American Music Award, multiple New York Music Awards, received New York Music Hall of Fame honors and ranked as the Number 18 Female Dance Artist of all time by Rolling Stone magazine. She is one of the very few artists in musical history to successfully crossover into almost every musical genre and chart with hit singles in Pop, Dance, R&B, Adult contemporary, and Rock. Exposé Get ready for a night of unforgettable 80s and 90s dance-pop when Exposé takes the stage. This legendary female vocal group from Miami helped define the freestyle and dance-pop sound of the late 1980s and early 1990s, delivering a string of iconic hits that still fill dance floors today. With powerhouse vocals and timeless melodies, Exposé became the first group ever to score four Top 10 hits from their debut album, including classics like “Come Go With Me,” “Point of No Return,” “Let Me Be the One,” and their No. 1 smash hit “Seasons Change.” Featuring the celebrated voices of Jeanette Jurado, Ann Curless, and Gioia Bruno, Exposé continues to tour and delight fans worldwide with their signature blend of high-energy dance anthems and heartfelt ballads. From chart-topping radio hits to beloved freestyle club favorites, Exposé delivers a show packed with nostalgia, powerful vocals, and infectious rhythms that will have audiences singing along all night. Join us for an electrifying evening celebrating one of the most successful girl groups of the 80s and a sound that defined a generation.
events.location: Canyons Village, Canyons Village- 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City, UT 84098
events.address: Canyons Village, Canyons Village- 4000 Canyons Resort Dr, Park City, UT 84098

events.title: Stravinsky's Firebird Suite & String Trio "Time for Three
events.description: Presented by Deer Valley & Utah Symphony — Stravinsky’s Firebird Suite takes flight in this spectacular program led by Utah Symphony Music Director Designate Markus Poschner. First, GRAMMY®-winning trio Time for Three—known for their genre-defying blend of classical, Americana, and contemporary styles— takes the stage for Kevin Puts’ Contact, the winner of a GRAMMY® for Best Contemporary Classical Composition. Written specifically for the group by the Pulitzer Prize-winning composer, the work explores themes of human connection and highlights the trio’s dynamic, longtime partnership with the Utah Symphony. Then, hear one of the most thrilling pieces ever written echoing through the Wasatch Mountains as the orchestra takes you on a journey from the explosive “Infernal Dance” to the powerful finale of Stravinsky’s Firebird. Gates Open: 6 p.m. | Show Time: 7:30 p.m.

events.title: Best Day Ever
events.description: “Best Day Ever” follows adaptive mountain bikers Greg Durso and Allie Bianchi as they tackle the daily challenges of disability—and find joy, connection, and belonging in Vermont’s mountain biking community. Set in the Green Mountains, this is a story of grit, independence, and what’s possible when community and creativity come together. At its heart is the world’s first fully adaptive trail network—built to prove that everyone belongs outside. Visually stunning and emotionally powerful, “Best Day Ever” challenges perceptions of disability and celebrates community. **Post-film Q&A with Greg Durso. Reception to follow in Community Room.** **Closed Captions are available for the film and ASL interpretation will be provided for the post-film discussions upon request. Please contact director@parkcityfilm.org by July 15th with ADA accommodation requests.**...

events.description: Experience the flavors of the mountains at Silver Lake, where open-fire cooking meets alpine views. Led by Overland Cookery's expedition chefs, this weekend draws inspiration from cooking in remote environments. Open fire techniques and thoughtful preparation create an experience that feels both elevated and grounded. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, it is an easy, social setting to spend the afternoon, where food, fire, an d the outdoors come together. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from Chef Peter Menteer's showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to Chef Sam Phelan's plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission is free with lift access from Snow Park. Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate....

events.title: Sawyer Brown
events.description: “We don’t have a lot of love songs. We have life songs.” – Mark Miller For more than 40 years, Sawyer Brown has delivered high-energy, blue-collar country-rock that keeps audiences on their feet. From winning Star Search to sharing stages with legends like Kenny Rogers and Dolly Parton, they’ve built a career defined by nonstop touring and unforgettable live shows. With 51 charted singles—including the #1 hit “Some Girls Do”—and over 6,000 performances worldwide, Sawyer Brown continues to bring the same grassroots spirit and electrifying presence that made them a household name. Skylar Geer Experience an evening of authentic country music with Utah singer-songwriter Skylar Geer, a talented performer known for his rich vocals, heartfelt songwriting, and energetic live shows. Blending traditional country roots with Southern rock influences, Skylar creates a sound that is both timeless and fresh, connecting with audiences through stories of life, love, and the American West. After spending several years in Nashville honing his craft as a songwriter and performer, Skylar returned to Utah and has become a favorite on stages throughout the Mountain West. Backed by a talented band featuring fiddle, guitars, bass, and drums, his performances seamlessly mix original music with crowd-favorite country classics and modern hits, all delivered with his signature style.

events.title: Circular Garden
events.description: *Circular Garden* brings together botanical paintings by Carol O'Malia and abstract pointillist works by AJ Oishi in a vibrant exploration of nature, color, and inner transformation. In her newest series, O’Malia reinterprets the lush floral compositions of Rachel Ruysch, the celebrated 17th- and 18th-century Dutch painter known for her exquisite still lifes. While honoring Ruysch’s extraordinary detail and layered arrangements, O’Malia introduces a contemporary sensibility through luminous natural backgrounds and subtle abstract elements that breathe new life into the tradition of botanical painting. Oishi’s serene, meditative paintings are composed of thousands of carefully placed dots and harmonious complementary colors. The recurring circular forms throughout her work symbolize individuality, growth, and the ongoing journey toward a more mindful and balanced life....
events.location: Julie Nester Gallery, Park City, UT

events.description: Greetings Volunteers! We're looking forward to working with you and would like to share details about our upcoming dig day. We'll be meeting at the private parking area directly west of the Bonanza Flat parking lot at 5pm \(no fee to park\). We'll then head out to do dirt work on the new hiking trail connecting 9k and Mid Mountain. If you're a mountain biker wondering why you should be doing work on a hike-only trail, please remember that these options alleviate traffic and help to optimize bike-only options throughout the system. We strive to make our volunteer days as productive and fulfilling as possible. This project presents an excellent opportunity to create a unique legacy trail that will benefit you and the community for years to come! Timeframe: 5:00pm Meet at the trailhead 5:15pm Head to worksite 8:00pm Return to trailhead Waivers are required every year, for each participant, and MUST be filled out beforehand: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdGpEehj4t8X4AkxwyseMgjIzErC6p2WDGm1lWhIxUdKM6DiQ/viewform?vc=0&c=0&w=1&flr=0 For your comfort and safety, please come prepared with the following: Water Sun Protection Gloves Snacks Closed-Toe Footwear Dig-day events are RSVP only. Please be sure to mark "Going" to the Facebook event so that we can accommodate you. If you have any questions, please contact Ian Hartley : Ian@mountaintrails.org...

events.title: Movement & Mindfulness Wed Trail Hike Series (Pt. 1)
events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. MORE DETAILS \>\> Trail time is calling → SIGN UP NOW DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/workshops Reserve your spot today: wildwomentribe.net/registration-2 VENMO: Sliding scale -- $40 to $55 suggested contribution \(@wildwomentribe -- last 4 digits 9583; do NOT select purchase protection or it'll deduct a fee you'll be asked to cover\)...

events.title: Shakespeare’s Legacy: From the Globe to Grand Opera lecture by Ben Beckman
events.description: Park City Museum will host a lecture called *Shakespeare’s Legacy: From the Globe to Grand Opera* by Ben Beckman on Wednesday, July 29, 2026 from 5-6 p.m. held at the Education and Collections Center located at 2079 Sidewinder Drive. More information can be found at www.parkcityhistory.org. William Shakespeare’s influence extends far beyond the stage: over 400 years after his death, his plays continue to be read and performed around the world, and adaptations of his work in film, television, musical theater, and more abound. The Bard also leaves a deep legacy in the field of opera, inspiring favorites in the operatic canon by composers like Verdi, Berlioz, Purcell, Barber – and Gounod. Ahead of Park City Opera’s production of Gounod’s *Roméo et Juliette*, join us to explore Shakespeare’s enduring legacy in opera, the history of Shakespeare performance in Utah, and the story behind Gounod’s masterpiece. Benjamin Beckman is a composer, conductor, and pianist who serves as the Artistic Director of Park City Opera. He studied at Yale and the University of Southern California, and has worked with opera companies around America, including The Atlanta Opera, Sarasota Opera, Festival Napa Valley, Pacific Opera Project, The Opera Guild of Palm Springs, Opera Company of Middlebury Vermont, and Chicago Summer Opera. He will be conducting Park City Opera's production of Gounod’s *Roméo et Juliette* at the Eccles Center in Downtown Park City on August 21st and 23rd....
events.location: Education and Collections Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Volunteer Weed Pull
events.description: Join the Swaner Preserve & EcoCenter in this outdoor project pulling invasive weeds! Invasive weeds crowd out native species and impair habitat. This is a great way to get onto areas of the Preserve that are not open to the public. Volunteers should be able to walk over uneven ground, bend over for extended periods, work outdoors in a variety of conditions, and comply with the safety guidelines. MEETING LOCATION TBD based on plant growth. Tools and supplies are provided. Volunteers will learn invasive weed identification as well as methods for successful removal. Kids as young as 10 may accompany their parent or guardian for the duration of the project, but keep in mind the strenuous nature of the project. Unaccompanied minors must be 14+. If you have any questions regarding this, contact Rhea at rhea.cone@usu.edu....
events.location: Swaner EcoCenter, Park City, UT

events.title: Mozart, Prokofiev, & More
events.description: Mozart's gripping Overture to Don Giovanni opens the concert with ominous chords and cleverly placed silence, building tension before racing forward with exhilarating momentum. Prokofiev&\#03's sparkling Sinfonietta follows, bursting with playful rhythms colored by the composer&\#03's youthful wit and charm. The program concludes with Louise Farrenc's Symphony No. 3, an expressive work admired in her lifetime by musicians including Schumann and Berlioz for its elegant structure and dramatic range, moving between brooding drama and romantic warmth.

events.title: (Re)sounding: Art Exhibition
events.description: Sound is not simply something we hear, but something we inhabit, shape, and are shaped by. Through immersive audio installations, evocative visual translations of sound, and interactive artworks that invite participation, the artists in (Re)sounding explore sound as a dynamic subject and material. This exhibition is an invitation to listen more deeply—to feel the weight and resonance of sound, to dwell inside it, and to hear the world in new ways.

events.description: David Young & The Interstate Kings are a three piece Roots-Rock group serving up vintage American music with . Formed in a small town in Maine in 2015, the band consists of David Young on guitar/vocals, Deven Young on drums, and Seth Martin on bass. After initial success in the Maine scene including appearances on Maine television staples such as 207 and The Nite Show, The Interstate Kings refocused their desire to bring their brand of bluesy, crunchy Americana to a wider audience and moved to Nashville, TN, where they're currently based.

events.title: movies under the stars: the road to patagonia
events.description: MOVIES UNDER THE STARS: the road to patagonia Tuesday, August 4th | Gates 7:30 pm, LIVE MUSIC BY JIM FISH STARTING AT 7:30 pm | Movie at 8:30 pm | Alta Peruvian Back Lawn│$15 | Tickets | Kids Under 5 are FREE THE ROAD TO PATAGONIA Is a stunning, intimate and unflinching series of love letters within a documentary - firstly, a love between two people, and secondly between humanity and the Earth. Ecologist Matty Hannon begins an incredible solo adventure, to surf the west coast of the Americas by motorbike, from the top of Alaska to the tip of Patagonia. But deep in the wilderness - alone with the wolves and the bears - the journeyer's plans unexpectedly fall to pieces. After losing everything, and on the cusp of quitting, he meets the girl of his dreams, a permaculture farmer named Heather. Shot over 16 years, the result is an adventurous exposé on the more-than-human-world, offering a physical and spiritual odyssey to better understand our place in Nature. Winner - Best Film and Best Documentary - Byron Bay International Film Festival Winner Audience Choice Award - Florida Surf Film Festival Winner Audience Choice Award - Melbourne Documentary Film Festival FACTS & TIPS: You must bring your own LOW chairs and they must be 31.5" height or less. Or blankets to sit on. Picnic dinners are allowed. NO GRILLS. If two separate households would like to be near each other we recommend arriving at the same time. No dogs, unless tagged by the Town of Alta or an ADA service dog. Prepare for chilly mountain evenings, bring blankets and layers. If you have questions regarding accessibility to our events, please contact Sara Gibbs, Executive Director. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: fearless abstract painting
events.description: fearless abstract painting Tuesday , August 18th | 6PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $15 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to create fearless, intuitive abstract paintings using Samantha da Silva's signature "no rules" roller-painting method, incorporating plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and playful canvas manipulation techniques. This class is especially good for people who want to learn how to silence their inner critic, overcome fear of the blank canvas, trust their intuition, and discover their own authentic artistic voice in a joyful, judgment-free space. You will use rollers \(instead of brushes\), large quantities of acrylic paint and water, plaster, salt, sand, sawdust, and your hands directly on the canvas laid flat on a table. At the end of the class, you will leave with one completed, textured abstract painting, a toolbox of fearless techniques you can use forever, and the confidence to keep creating without rules or self-doubt. Samantha da Silva \(b. 1978, Brazil\) is a Brazilian-born, Salt Lake City-based artist and dedicated educator who has taught visual arts for over twenty years in public and private schools and institutions across Canada, the United States, Hawaii, and France. Shaped by nearly forty moves triggered by natural disasters and life circumstances, and by the intense assimilation she experienced at age ten, da Silva channels themes of migration, belonging, and resourcefulness into her widely recognized large-scale relief sculptures-topographic-like works built from found dirt, sawdust, and debris-that have appeared in Architectural Digest, Bravo TV, and HGTV. In the classroom, she guides students of all ages to transform personal experience and everyday materials into powerful expression, making art a vital tool for connection and resilience in an ever-shifting world. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions...

events.title: The Splinter Beneath a Fractured Star: Moses Williams & Chaz Prymek
events.description: About Artists Moses Williams and Chaz Prymek collaborate on an immersive sound-and-video performance. Blending original composition with moving image, the piece invites viewers to engage with space, time, and perception in new and unexpected ways. About Moses Williams Moses lives in Salt Lake City, Utah, and is a professor of sculpture and intermedia at the University of Utah. Moses received a BFA from Watkins College of Art, Design & Film in Nashville, TN, and an MFA from Carnegie-Mellon University in Pittsburgh, PA. He has exhibited and performed throughout the United States and Europe, including Apexart - New York, SeelenArt Galerie, Munich, The Andy Warhol Museum, Carnegie Museum of Art, Zeihersmith Gallery, Das KloHäuschen Art Center, Der Kulturanker Magdeburg, Utah Museum of Contemporary Art, Utah Museum of Fine Art, and David B Smith Gallery. Moses has collaborated with The Nashville Ballet and Alias Chamber Ensemble, and with the residents of Unit 2 \(the Death Row unit\) at Riverbend Maximum Security Institution in Nashville, TN, on a project with multiple exhibitions spanning several years. About Chaz Prymek Chaz Prymek is a guitarist, interdisciplinary artist and curator working and living in Salt Lake City, UT. Perhaps most widely known for his work under the moniker Lake Mary or as member of the ‘ambient jazz' quartet Fuubutsushi, Prymek makes work that bridges fingerstyle guitar, ambient, folk, jazz and free improvisation, making music and art that delve into the spaciousness of deep emotional narratives and personal hymn. Prymek's sonic exploratory nature has resulted in nearly three dozen solo and collaborative releases, film scores, music for dance and art installations and has appeared on numerous records. Prymek also curates and facilitates Yardwork Presents, an exploratory music and arts programming throughout Salt Lake City, and is co-founder of Profane Illuminations Editions, a small editions imprint fostering re-enchantment in everyday life...
events.location: Kimball Art Center, 1251 Kearns Boulevard, Park City, UT 84060, USA

events.title: Psychedelic Furs with Special Guest Living Colour
events.description: The Psychedelic Furs may not have invented rock & roll per se, but their influence since arriving on the post-punk scorched-earth landscape four decades ago has reverberated and resonated among all those who cherish the sweet-and-sour spot where rawness and romanticism meet. Born out of the UK post-punk scene and led by the vocalist and songwriter Richard Butler, and his bass-wielding brother Tim, the Furs quickly developed as one of the premiere bands at college and alternative radio, scoring a flurry of major hits with "Love My Way," "Pretty In Pink," "Heaven," "The Ghost In You, “And “Heartbreak Beat” in all releasing eight studio albums, spawning several compilations, a boxed set, a live concert DVD, and inspiring one of the most iconic motion picture soundtracks of all time. Decades later, their latest release, “Made Of Rain” charted in a multitude of countries and became the Furs’ Second-highest-ranked UK Album ever. It was prominently featured at the end of the year “Best Albums” chart roundup in dozens of publications worldwide. But the Furs especially thrive live in concert, having headlined at the U.K.’s famed Glastonbury Festival, and more recently at esteemed venues including the Hollywood Bowl, London’s Royal Albert Hall, New York’s Radio City Music Hall,and Nashville’s Grand ‘le Opry, among others. With a dazzling live show, they continue to tour quite regularly across the globe Living Colour Few bands have reshaped the sound and spirit of rock music like Living Colour. Formed in New York City in 1984 and led by visionary guitarist Vernon Reid, the band—Corey Glover \(vocals\), Will Calhoun \(drums\), and Doug Wimbish \(bass\)—delivers a powerful fusion of rock, funk, jazz, and metal that remains as bold and relevant today as ever. Living Colour burst onto the scene with their groundbreaking debut album Vivid \(1988\), which climbed to \#6 on the Billboard 200 and earned double-platinum status. The album featured the iconic anthem “Cult of Perso...

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Reception & Five-Course Dinner
events.description: The Taste of Luxury Series featuring Stag's Leap — Deer Valley’s Taste of Luxury Series continues with a celebration honoring the 50th anniversary of the 1976 Judgment of Paris, the landmark tasting that forever changed the global perception of California wine and established Napa Valley as a world-class wine region. To commemorate the occasion, Deer Valley welcomes Marcus Notaro, head winemaker of Stag's Leap Wine Cellars, alongside returning guest Chef Clement Gelas of Talisker Club for an intimate evening at Deer Valley Café. Set pond-side at sunset, guests will enjoy a thoughtfully curated five-course pairing experience inspired by the wines, stories, and legacy behind this groundbreaking moment in wine history. RESERVE YOUR TICKETS EVENT SCHEDULE Meet-and-Greet Reception 6:30 p.m. Five-Course Dinner & Wine Pairing 7 p.m.

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 1 and 2, Chef Ryan Arensdorf of KC Que takes the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from a showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission to the event is free with the purchase of a lift ticket . Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Boney James
events.description: Saxophonist, Producer and Composer Boney James’ New Album ‘Slow Burn’ slow burn: noun, informal A feeling that grows with a slow but deliberate intensity. For saxophonist and composer Boney James, these words perfectly describe the music he has crafted for his new Concord Records album, Slow Burn, to be released on October 18, 2024. “When I mentioned that phrase to people as a possible title, they said, “That also refers to your whole career in a way” James says with a laugh. “This is my 19th record and I feel like I’m just hitting my stride.” Slow Burn is a new high point in a remarkable career that now spans more than three decades and includes a long list of accolades; four GRAMMY nominations, three NAACP Image Award nominations, a Soul Train Award for Best Jazz Album and four RIAA Gold Record certifications. In 2009, Billboard named James one of the Top 3 Contemporary Jazz Artists of the decade. In 2024 year Boney became the first ever artist to score 20 number one singles on the Billboard Smooth Jazz chart. But Boney James has never been one to look backwards and Slow Burn, which the artist produced, marks several firsts. Beginning with the album’s opening track, ‘Arcadia.’ The very first notes on the album are the acoustic bass playing of the legendary Marcus Miller. It’s one of the few times that Miller, known for his electric bass guitar work, has committed his standup bass playing to a recording and the first time that James has incorporated an upright bass into his own music Next is James’ intriguing interpretation of the classic ‘Butterfly.’ Co-written by master keyboardist Herbie Hancock and Bennie Maupin, the track features Marcus Miller again as well as young lion Cory Henry’s inspired electric piano. “This beautiful melody got stuck in my head” explains James. “I have never covered a tune by a legend like Herbie before and I hope we did him justice.” The title track, ‘Slow Burn,’ is one of four tunes on the album that James co-wrote an

events.description: Breathe deeper. Move freer. Connect stronger. Wild Women Tribe's fall Movement & Mindfulness Wed Weekly Trail Hike Series is back for the 4th season \(Wed 7/1 to Wed 8/5\)—six weeks of soul-fueled hikes, fresh air, and movement that awakens more than just your body. It’s not just about the hike. It’s about the women walking beside you. The conversations that crack you open. The moments of presence & wonder. And the laughter that lingers long after the trail ends. Join our six-part hike series in Park City. We lace up and hit the dirt with a variety of modalities featuring local facilitators: from grounding somatic breathwork with Jilana McIntyre \(July 1\), a sacred blue lotus tea ceremony with Christina Sthair \(July 8\), to soul-soothing soundbath on the earth with Amanda Murphy \(July 29\), there's a little something for every woman seeking deep connection back to nature. July 1: Jilana McIntyre \(somatic breathwork\) July 8: Christina Sthair \(blue lotus tea ceremony\) July 15: Sofia Mileti \(Foundation training\) July 22: Renee Huang \(NLP style guided journal writing\) July 29: Amanda Murphy \(soundbath meditation\) Aug 5: Jai Nico \(Kundalini yoga activation\) Can't make our kick off? There are five other dates filling up fast with community members looking to connect on the trails. We're hiking and moving weekly until Wed Aug 5. events.title: Haydn, Mendelssohn, & A Viola Concerto
events.description: The rich voice of the viola takes center stage as Utah Symphony Principal Viola Brant Bayless performs Dai Fujikura’s Wayfinder, a concerto inspired by Polynesian navigation traditions. In this imaginative work, the orchestra becomes the natural forces used to find direction—stars, winds, and ocean swells—while the soloist listens and charts a path forward through shifting musical currents. Like a musical guide through the evening, Maurice Cohn conducts a program that unfolds across vivid sonic landscapes, beginning with Mendelssohn’s atmospheric The Hebrides, which evokes Scotland’s rugged coastline with dark, rolling lines in the low strings set against shimmering winds and strings that suggest the sea air. The journey concludes with Haydn’s jubilant Symphony No. 104—his final symphony—whose finale bursts with rustic, folk-inspired energy that resonates beautifully in the intimate acoustics of St. Mary’s Catholic Church....

events.title: Mark Chesnutt
events.description: Mark Chesnutt is one of country music’s most iconic voices, known for his unmistakable honky-tonk sound and a catalog of timeless hits including “Too Cold at Home,” “Brother Jukebox,” and “I Don’t Want to Miss a Thing.” A defining artist of the ’90s country era, Chesnutt earned 14 No. 1 singles, 23 Top 10 hits, over 12 million albums sold, and the CMA Horizon Award, with Billboard naming him one of the most-played country artists of the decade. Now energized and fully focused on performing, Chesnutt continues to deliver powerful, authentic live shows that celebrate classic country at its finest. Love & Chaos Bringing together the heart of Texas country, the soul of Americana, and the energy of country rock, Love & Chaos delivers a powerful live music experience that is equal parts authentic storytelling and high-octane entertainment. Known for their tight harmonies, driving guitars, and engaging stage presence, the band has built a loyal following throughout the Southwest and beyond. Drawing inspiration from the rich traditions of Red Dirt and Texas country music, Love & Chaos combines thoughtful songwriting with a dynamic sound that ranges from heartfelt ballads to crowd-pleasing anthems. Their performances showcase both original music and fan-favorite covers, creating an atmosphere that keeps audiences singing, dancing, and coming back for more.

events.title: not your grandma's tie dye
events.description: not your grandma's tie dye Thursday, August 6th | 6PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $10 | Sign-up Required Give an old t-shirt or two new life! In this hands-on class, you'll learn the basics of how to tie dye black or dark-colored clothing. You'll learn to tie your shirts into various patterns, then use bleach to lighten the fabric to prepare it for dye. You'll learn how to prevent the bleach from putting holes in your clothing, and then use professional-grade dyes to create your own custom color palette. You'll leave with speciality detergent and instructions to thoroughly wash your tie dye at home. Note: Using bleach to remove color is optional! You can bring white or light-colored items to this class. IMPORTANT: Please bring your own clothing to dye! Please limit yourself to 3 small items, like t-shirts or shorts, OR 1 large item, like a sweatshirt. MOST IMPORTANT: Items MUST be 100% cotton for best results. 80% cotton will work, but is less ideal. We do not recommend bringing clothing items that are less than 70% cotton. If you are unsure about an item, we recommend bringing something else. For color removal, the brand Comfort Colors is NOT recommended! The Trash Pile offers sustainably sourced, one-of-a-kind tie-dye. Each item is carefully selected at a local thrift store and is then thoughtfully tied and dyed by hand into a wearable work of art. Since launching in 2021, The Trash Pile has diverted more than 5,000 pieces of clothing from landfills. What began as a pandemic hobby is now a growing movement to save the planet, one t-shirt at a time. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Michael Kessler: All Resin
events.description: Michael Kessler has spent decades exploring the boundary between gesture and geometry, and his resin works bring that inquiry into sharper focus than ever. Each painting is built from up to fifty micro-thin translucent layers, fused and accumulated until something luminous emerges. Biomorphic forms weave through geometric structures, nature's logic made visible through an almost meditative process of application and restraint. Kessler likens his gestural freedom to a kind of painterly tai chi, controlled, but never rigid. In his all-resin works, that layered depth takes on new dimension. Light travels through the surface rather than simply reflecting off it, making each piece feel as much like a living object as a painting. Join us August 7th to experience new works by Michael Kessler at Gallery MAR, Park City....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Books & Mediumship
events.description: Join us for an inspiring afternoon of insight, connection, and Spirit with local Author Kim S. Bantle and International Medium Carm OSullivan! Have you ever wondered if your loved ones in Spirit are still with you? Have you experienced signs, synchronicities, or moments that couldn’t quite be explained? Join us for a heartfelt afternoon celebrating intuition, personal awakening, and the enduring connection between this world and the next. The afternoon begins with local **author Kim S. Bantle**, who will share the remarkable story behind her new book, **Confessions of a Closeted Clairvoyant.** Kim’s honest, humorous, and inspiring journey explores what it’s like to awaken to intuitive abilities later in life while learning to trust the guidance that has always been available. Following Kim’s presentation, **international evidential medium Carm O’Sullivan** will take the stage for a live demonstration of mediumship. Through evidential mediumship, Carm shares meaningful and specific evidence from loved ones in Spirit, offering messages filled with love, healing, hope, and validation. While not everyone will receive a personal reading, many attendees find that the messages shared resonate deeply and provide comfort, encouragement, and inspiration for their own lives. This event is perfect for anyone who: Is curious about mediumship and life after death. Has lost a loved one and is seeking hope and healing. Wants to learn more about intuition and spiritual awakening. Enjoys uplifting conversations that inspire personal growth. Loves books that encourage living with greater awareness and purpose. This will be an afternoon of laughter, heartfelt stories, and powerful moments that remind us one simple truth: **Love never dies.** Kim will have signed copies of *Confessions of a Closeted Clairvoyant* available for purchase following the presentation, with time to meet the author and ask questions. For more information and a link to Amazon, go to www.kimsbant...

events.title: Concerts on the Slopes
events.description: Lonestar is a celebrated American country music group that captured global attention with their timeless ballad “Amazed,” a crossover sensation that spent 9 weeks at No. 1 on the country charts and 2 weeks at No. 1 on the pop charts. Known for blending heartfelt storytelling with a polished pop-country sound, the band—comprised of Dean Sams, Michael Britt, Keech Rainwater, and Drew Womack—has left an indelible mark on the genre boasting a staggering 22 top ten singles on the Billboard Hot Country Songs chart, including 10 that reached No. 1. Their meteoric rise began in 1995 with the top 5 single “Tequila Talking,” followed by No. 1 hits like the rock-tinged “No News,” “Come Cryin’ to Me,” and “Everything’s Changed.” The release of their quadruple-platinum 1999 album Lonely Grill cemented their legacy, delivering four No. 1 singles and showcasing their knack for emotionally resonant, radiofriendly songs. Lonestar’s success has earned them widespread acclaim and numerous accolades, including Academy of Country Music awards for New Vocal Group, Single and Song of the Year, and Humanitarian of the Year. They were also honored with the Country Music Association’s Vocal Group of the Year and International Artist Achievement Award. With more than 10.5 million records sold worldwide, Lonestar stands as one of country music’s most enduring and influential bands, continuing to inspire fans with their heartfelt hits and musical legacy....

events.title: Labor Day Experiences
events.description: Celebrate the close of summer with a long weekend designed for easygoing fun, outdoor play, and family-friendly moments. From lively daytime activities to relaxed evening experiences, Labor Day at Deer Valley offers the perfect setting to soak in the season’s final days. **DALY’S DINING & GAMES** ***Daly’s Pub & Rec*** **September 4 – 6 | 2:00pm – 9:00pm** Relax and recharge at Daly’s Pub & Rec, where casual dining meets interactive entertainment. Open throughout the afternoon and evening, Daly’s offers approachable fare, handcrafted beverages, bowling lanes, and arcade games, making it an easygoing destination to gather and enjoy the holiday weekend. **ANIMAL SHOW** ***Compass Sports*** **September 4 | 3:00pm** Meet fascinating animals native to Utah and hear engaging stories from their caretakers during this interactive wildlife presentation. Designed to educate and entertain, the animal show offers a memorable experience for guests of all ages. **CANDY LAND** ***5th Floor*** **September 4 – 6 | 3:00pm – 4:30pm** Children are invited to explore a curated candy station and select their favorite sweets to create a personalized assortment. A simple, playful activity designed especially for younger guests, Candy Land offers a cheerful way to enjoy a sweet moment during Labor Day weekend. **CHILDREN’S NIGHT OUT** ***Paintbox*** **September 4 & 5 | 5:00pm – 9:00pm** While parents enjoy an evening during Labor Day weekend, younger guests are invited to spend the night at Paintbox for a fun and thoughtfully curated experience. Children’s Night Out features engaging craft projects, a family-friendly movie, and a relaxed dinner, all guided by Montage Deer Valley’s trained team. Designed to feel playful, comfortable, and welcoming, the evening offers children a memorable night of creativity and connection while parents enjoy time of their own. *Reservations required.* **VEUVE CLICQUOT APRÈS LOUNGE: SUNSET SERIES** ***Après Lounge*** **September 4 & 5 ...

events.title: Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7
events.description: Promising a night of lyrical beauty and orchestral drama, conductor Louis Lohraseb shapes a program that follows an arc of adoration to heartbreak in the first half. The season finale at St. Mary’s Catholic Church opens with Wagner’s tender Siegfried Idyll, a deeply personal expression of love written as a birthday gift for his wife. Then the spotlight turns to Erica Petrocelli in two dramatic works that showcase the brilliance and expressive power of the soprano voice: Mozart’s virtuosic “Come scoglio” from Così fan tutte, a declaration of unwavering fidelity, and Mendelsohn's “Infelice,” where sweet memories offer fleeting comfort after a lover’s betrayal. Famously called “the apotheosis of the dance itself” by Wagner, Beethoven’s Symphony No. 7 brings the night to a triumphant close. An instant symphonic favorite—with early audiences reportedly demanding a repeat of the second movement—the piece surges with wildness and joy, culminating in one of the most electrifying finales in classical music....

events.title: Walker Hayes
events.description: Country superstar Walker Hayes is a singer-songwriter from Mobile, Alabama, known for blending country storytelling with pop and hip-hop-inspired production. His breakout smash “Fancy Like” topped country sales and streaming charts, spent over six months at No. 1 on Billboard’s Hot Country Songs chart, reached the Top 5 on the Billboard Hot 100, hit No. 1 at country radio, and earned Hayes his first GRAMMY nomination for Best Country Song. The viral hit was also featured in a nationwide Applebee’s commercial. With additional fan favorites like “AA,” “Y’all Life,” and “U Gurl,” Hayes has become one of the top-selling and most-streamed artists in modern country music. His latest project, 17 Problems, is his most personal work to date—an introspective album shaped by resilience, faith, family, grief, and growth. Featuring both high-energy anthems and deeply emotional storytelling, the album highlights Hayes’ evolution as an artist and songwriter. Known for his authenticity and engaging live performances, Hayes has appeared on Good Morning America, The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon, Late Night with Seth Meyers, TODAY, and CBS Sunday Morning. Whether delivering chart-topping hits or stripped-down acoustic moments, Walker Hayes invites audiences into a powerful, honest, and unforgettable live experience. Wildwood Get ready for an evening of crowd-pleasing country music with Wildwood, one of Utah’s fastest-rising country acts. Led by powerhouse vocalists Chad Truman and Kate Miller, Wildwood delivers a dynamic blend of modern country hits, classic favorites, and original songs, all brought to life with rich harmonies, exceptional musicianship, and infectious energy. Known for their engaging stage presence and ability to get audiences singing and dancing along, Wildwood has performed throughout the Mountain West, sharing stages with nationally recognized country artists and earning praise for their authentic sound and high-energy performances. events.title: Concerts on the Slopes
events.description: Walker Hayes returns with his most introspective and heartfelt project yet, 17 Problems, an album shaped by reflection, resilience, and raw honesty. Blending hip-hop-infused production with country storytelling, Hayes dives deep into themes of fatherhood, faith, grief, and growth. From the aching vulnerability of “17 Year Old Problems” and “Song for My Son” to the high-energy anthems “Mother” and “Neon Lights,” the album captures the full spectrum of life’s beauty and messiness. With over 100 songs written for this release, Hayes carefully curated 17 tracks that reveal the most personal side of his artistry to date, working with top collaborators like Warren “Oak” Felder, Shane McAnally, and Bart Millard while also penning deeply personal solo material. To bring this story to life, Hayes has announced the Walker Hayes Unplugged Tour, where he offers fans an intimate, acoustic experience unlike anything he’s done before. Stripping back the production to let the lyrics lead, Hayes takes audiences on a journey through the highs and lows that shaped him - from viral fame to quiet family moments, from deep heartbreak to enduring faith. Through heartfelt storytelling and soul-stirring songs, this special evening invites fans into the heart of his story. More than a concert, it’s a celebration of connection, resilience, and the messy, beautiful reality of life, one song at a time....
events.location: Canyons Village at Park City, Park City, UT

Join icebreaker , Deer Valley's Official Baselayer and Midlayer Partner, for a complimentary guided hike along New York State of Mine Trail. Enjoy scenic mountain views, fresh alpine air, and a fun outdoor experience designed for hikers of all ages. This family-friendly hike lasts approximately one hour and is limited to 20 guests per session. Following the hike, each participant will receive a gift from icebreaker. events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from a showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission to the event is free with the purchase of a lift ticket . Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

Fox School of Wine invites you to a fun-filled, guided wine tasting in Park CityExecutive Sommelier and Fox School of Wine Headmistress, Kirsten Fox, and her Wine Professors will present a well-paced tasting of five, approachable, under-$45 hidden gem wines that you may have overlooked in the aisles at your local liquor store.You'll receive a tasting pour of each wine, stories and history about the winery, grapes, and region, food pairing ideas... plus our signature Blind Tasting Challenge, when we pour you a taste of a wine you've already explored in class to see if you can guess which one it is. With Ali Campbell’s unmistakable voice leading the way, UB40 has delivered some of the most beloved songs of all time, including Red Red Wine, Can’t Help Falling in Love, Kingston Town, Here I Am (Come and Take Me), and Food for Thought. Their music has defined generations, blending reggae rhythms with pop sensibility and global appeal. Set against the stunning backdrop of Canyons Village at Park City Mountain, Concerts on the Slopes offers a one-of-a-kind outdoor concert experience. Enjoy cool mountain temperatures, sweeping alpine views, and an energetic yet intimate setting with 5,000 seats and top-tier sound and production. Make it a full Park City experience—arrive early, explore the village, and settle in for an unforgettable summer evening filled with timeless music, mountain air, and good vibes. events.title: Better Than Ezra with Special Guests Vertical Horizon and TONIC
events.description: Get ready for a night of iconic ’90s and early-2000s alternative rock as Better Than Ezra and Vertical Horizon take the stage together for an unforgettable live show. Better Than Ezra, known for their chart-topping hits like “Good,” “Desperately Wanting,” and “King of New Orleans,” rose to prominence during the alternative rock boom of the 1990s. With their melodic hooks, thoughtful lyrics, and energetic live performances, the band has remained a fan favorite for decades. Joining them is Vertical Horizon, the multi-platinum band behind massive radio hits “Everything You Want,” “You’re a God,” and “Best I Ever Had.” Their emotionally driven songwriting and polished rock sound helped define an era of modern alternative and continue to resonate with audiences today. Together, these two beloved bands deliver a hit-packed night of nostalgia, sing-along anthems, and timeless alternative rock. Whether you grew up with these songs or discovered them later, this show promises a high-energy, feel-good concert experience you won’t want to miss. Music has an undeniable way of bringing people together, creating connections that run as deep as family. That bond is at the heart of Tonic, the GRAMMY® Award–nominated, multi-platinum rock trio made up of Emerson Hart (vocals, guitar), Jeff Russo (lead guitar), and Dan Lavery (bass). Since forming in the mid-1990s, Tonic has built a lasting legacy through eloquent songwriting, lyrical honesty, and unforgettable melodies. Their debut album Lemon Parade went platinum and produced the iconic “If You Could Only See,” the #1 most-played rock song of 1997. The band went on to score six Top 10 singles, sell over 4 million records, and earn two GRAMMY® Award nominations, including for “Take Me As I Am.” More than two decades later, Tonic’s music continues to resonate across generations. Fans—young and old—still sing every word, proving the songs are bigger than time and bigger than the band itself. After brief breaks and successful solo caree

Singer/songwriter Cory Mon has a distinctly unique, albeit pleasant voice in the world of music. “Happy \(sometimes sad\), gritty, funky, folksy, bluesy, rocky music… I guess”… he says when asked the dreaded, “So what type of music do you play?” Assuming the life of a musician, Cory has thrived as well as endured through the many ups and downs that have come throughout his well tenured 20\+ year music career. Cory has released 60\+ original songs and toured with and/or supported a handful of his favorite artists: JJ Grey & Mofro, Sean Hayes, The Avett Brothers, Patterson Hood etc. www.corymon.com...

events.title: Maoli
events.description: Now breaking into the mainstream with a one-of-a-kind blend of island reggae and country soul, Hawaiian-born rising star Maoli is bringing his people—and his sound—to the world like never before. The golden-voiced native of upcountry Maui has released nine independent albums, earned over half a billion global streams, and spent nearly 15 years touring and recording, becoming a major fixture across the Pacific Islands and beyond. Inspired by traditional Hawaiian music (the birthplace of the steel guitar), reggae, rock, and country legends like Dolly Parton, Willie Nelson, and George Strait, Maoli has built a signature style that connects Polynesia to the American West Coast—and all the way to Texas. With a growing global fanbase, Maoli’s Hawaiian-country sound has fueled viral hits and powerful covers, including Brett Young’s “Mercy” (18+ million Spotify streams) and “In Case You Didn’t Know” (10+ million Spotify streams). Now teaming up with hit producers Dann and David Huff, Maoli continues bridging the gap between island soul and country tradition with his latest release, Last Sip of Summer (Island Vibe Edition)—out now.

events.description: **Four-part WANDER series focused on women’s hormonal health throughout the seasons (with 8 bonus monthly Zoom calls for monthly check ins)** ***Are fatigue, mood shifts, or hormonal changes dimming your spark?*** You’re not alone. Hormones shape every part of our lives—and they’re influenced by the seasons, both in nature and within ourselves. Whether you’re navigating postpartum recovery, fertility planning, perimenopause, postmenopause, or simply wanting more energy and clarity, this series will help you realign with your body’s wisdom and the earth’s cycles. Step into the unique rhythm of each season with Wild Women Tribe’s **Seasonal Hormone Series**, co-hosted by naturopathic physician Dr. Trevor Cates, in a powerful blend of nature, wellness and community. This transformative quarterly series invites you to connect deeply with nature’s cycles through intentional movement, seasonal nourishment, and a sisterhood community of other women who are committed to their natural health journey. events.title: lake effect: defending the greatest snow on earth panel discussion
events.description: lake effect: defending the greatest snow on earth panel discussion Thursday, August 27th | 6 PM Doors | 6:30 PM Start | Our Lady of the Snows | Free | Sign-up Required Join us for a panel discussion on the relationship between the Great Salt Lake and snowpack in the Wasatch Mountains. Presented by ACE, Torrey House Press, Grow the Flow, and Save Our Canyons, LAKE EFFECT is a call to understand the connection between the Great Salt Lake and the Wasatch Mountains and how we can defend the greatest snow on earth. Featuring Ayja Bounous and Teri Harman, writers and contributors to the anthology The Once and Future Lake: Stories for Great Salt Lake; Jake Dreyfous, managing director of Grow the Flow; and Jack Stauss, Executive Director of Save Our Canyons; and moderated by Torrey House Press Executive Director Kirsten Johanna Allen, LAKE EFFECT invites a community discussion on how to maintain a legacy of winter for future generations. ABOUT THE PANELISTS: Kirsten Johanna Allen \(she/her\) directs acquisitions, editing, production, and marketing for Torrey House Press with co-executive director Will Neville-Rehbehn. As co-founder, Kirsten has steered the press from its earliest inklings to publish conservation through literature to become the powerful platform for books and ideas about the natural world and the West that it is today. Kirsten holds a BA from Westminster College and an MPH from the University of Utah. She has two grown children and lives with a pair of cats and her spouse, Mark Bailey, in Salt Lake City and Torrey, Utah. Ayja Bounous is a Salt Lake City-based writer inspired by water in all its forms. She is the award-winning author of Junior Bounous and the Joys of Skiing and Shaped by Snow: Defending the Future of Winter, and a contributor to Alta and Little Cottonwood: Jewel of the Wasatch Mountains and The Once and Future Lake: Stories for Great Salt Lake. Ayja holds a master's in Environmental Humanities from the University of Utah and hosts Snow \+...

events.description: RICK SPRINGFIELD BIO: Over the past four decades, Rick Springfield has worn many hats as an entertainer and performer. The creator of some of the finest power-pop of the ’80s, a Grammy winning singer, songwriter, and musician who has sold 25 million albums and scored 17 U.S. Top 40 hits, including “Jessie’s Girl,” “Don’t Talk to Strangers,” “An Affair of the Heart,” "I've Done Everything for You,” “Love Somebody,” and “Human Touch.” He’s an accomplished actor who has starred opposite Meryl Streep in the feature film “Ricki and the Flash,” gave a chameleonic performance as the creepy Dr. Irving Pitlor in HBO’s prestige drama “True Detective,” earned great reviews for his portrayal of Lucifer on the CW hit “Supernatural” and most recently played the insane Pastor Charles on FX’s worldwide hit series “American Horror Story.” An author as well, both his candid 2010 memoir Late, Late at Night (which Rolling Stone named one of the 25 greatest rock memoirs of all time) and his 2014 comedic novel Magnificent Vibration earned rave reviews and spots on the New York Times Best Sellers’ list. In 2014, Springfield was honored with a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, located around the corner from the first apartment he lived in when he first arrived in the U.S. from Australia in 1971. Springfield’s Sirius-XM radio show, Working Class DJ, is a huge hit and airs weekly on 80s on 8. His new album “Automatic” is out now. events.title: Dylan Scott
events.description: ABOUT DYLAN SCOTT:Despite a multi-Platinum story and six No. 1 singles, including two fresh smashes in 2025 alone, Curb Records hitmaker Dylan Scott has always kept an underdog mindset. A rowdy roughneck with a romantic side – one whose modern edge was sharpened by timeless, emotional twang – the consummate family man broke out with the epic triple-Platinum devotion of “My Girl” in 2016 and followed up with scrappy, under-the-radar consistency. Chart-topping heart-forward anthems like “Hooked,” “Nobody” and “New Truck” all reached RIAA-certified status, but last year launched the crooner into the stratosphere. Posting back-to-back No. 1s with Gold-certified “Boys Back Home” (with Dylan Marlowe) and Platinum-certified “This Town’s Been Too Good to Us,” Scott wrapped 2025 as the third most-played country artist of 2025 and continues his hot streak with his current single, Platinum-certified “What He’ll Never Have,” which nears the Top 10 at Country radio and was honored by ASCAP as one of the most-performed country songs of 2025. With nearly 5 billion career streams, legions of fans and countless sold-out shows to his credit, his stardom has somehow grown in relative stealth. But with his third full album, Easy Does It, he’s not sweating. Sure of himself, his talent and his fans, Scott heads toward a new creative chapter with total confidence. Featuring 11 tracks, his signature vocal and vivid intensity take center stage on a set defined by personal truth – whether through heartbreak, romantic devotion or small-town dedication. The in-demand live act spent last year on his Country Till I Die Tour, headlining his biggest venues yet including his first-ever arenas and record-breaking attendance, and his Easy Does It Tour. He will continue headlining across the country on his Till I Can’t I Will Tour, launching this spring.
