events.title: Labor Day Experiences
events.description: Celebrate the close of summer with a long weekend designed for easygoing fun, outdoor play, and family-friendly moments. From lively daytime activities to relaxed evening experiences, Labor Day at Deer Valley offers the perfect setting to soak in the season’s final days. **DALY’S DINING & GAMES** ***Daly’s Pub & Rec*** **September 4 – 6 | 2:00pm – 9:00pm** Relax and recharge at Daly’s Pub & Rec, where casual dining meets interactive entertainment. Open throughout the afternoon and evening, Daly’s offers approachable fare, handcrafted beverages, bowling lanes, and arcade games, making it an easygoing destination to gather and enjoy the holiday weekend. **ANIMAL SHOW** ***Compass Sports*** **September 4 | 3:00pm** Meet fascinating animals native to Utah and hear engaging stories from their caretakers during this interactive wildlife presentation. Designed to educate and entertain, the animal show offers a memorable experience for guests of all ages. **CANDY LAND** ***5th Floor*** **September 4 – 6 | 3:00pm – 4:30pm** Children are invited to explore a curated candy station and select their favorite sweets to create a personalized assortment. A simple, playful activity designed especially for younger guests, Candy Land offers a cheerful way to enjoy a sweet moment during Labor Day weekend. **CHILDREN’S NIGHT OUT** ***Paintbox*** **September 4 & 5 | 5:00pm – 9:00pm** While parents enjoy an evening during Labor Day weekend, younger guests are invited to spend the night at Paintbox for a fun and thoughtfully curated experience. Children’s Night Out features engaging craft projects, a family-friendly movie, and a relaxed dinner, all guided by Montage Deer Valley’s trained team. Designed to feel playful, comfortable, and welcoming, the evening offers children a memorable night of creativity and connection while parents enjoy time of their own. *Reservations required.* **VEUVE CLICQUOT APRÈS LOUNGE: SUNSET SERIES** ***Après Lounge*** **September 4 & 5 ...
events.location: Montage Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Matt Flint: Sleeping in the Forest
events.description: Matt Flint doesn't begin with a plan. No sketches, no predetermined composition, just canvas, paint, and a process as intuitive as the landscapes that shaped him. Raised in rural Missouri and now living in Wyoming's Wind River Mountains, Flint has spent a lifetime in close study of the natural world, its textures, its rhythms, its constant state of becoming. That attention finds its way into every surface he paints. His works build through layers of creation and destruction, each image emerging as another recedes, holding the tension between what was and what might come next. The result is painting that moves the way wilderness does, familiar and elusive at once, inviting you closer while always suggesting there's something just beyond the next ridge. Join us September 5th to experience new works by Matt Flint at Gallery MAR, Park City....

events.title: create your own stacker rings
events.description: create your own stacker rings Thursday, September 10th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $25 | Sign-up Required In this hands-on workshop, you will have the opportunity to create TWO of your own stacker rings \(one sterling silver, one gold-filled\). You will learn how to measure, file, and solder your stackers. By the end of class, you'll leave with beautiful, handcrafted stackers you'll be proud to wear, plus the foundational metalsmithing skills to continue building your craft. No prior experience is necessary. You do not need to know your ring size prior. For ages 18 and up. With over 5 years of metalsmithing experience and a teaching background, teaching jewelry making is Megan's passion. She also teaches jewelry making at Salt Lake Community College and runs a small jewelry business, Crooked Trails Silver . Megan's teaching philosophy blends patience, creativity, and encouragement - whether you're a complete beginner or expanding your skills. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. events.title: Monster Drawing Rally
events.description: About Monster Drawing Rally is a live art-making fundraiser with local artists, benefiting the Arts Council of Park City & Summit County and Kimball Art Center. Monster Drawing Rally is a fundraising event benefiting the Arts Council of Park City & Summit County and Kimball Art Center. Held outdoors at Kimball Art Center, the event features live music, food, a cash bar, and live art-making sessions where artists create pieces in 50 minutes. Spectators can watch the creative process, build relationships with local artists, and purchase finished artwork on the spot. The event includes various mediums such as graphite, ink, charcoal, painting, digital art, and collage. If more than one person is interested in a piece, a random card draw will decide the buyer. Tickets are $25 for adults, $10 for kids aged 6-17, and free for children 5 and under. Get tickets. Be part of Monster as a volunteer! Click here to see all available positions. Are you an interested artist? Arts Council of Park City & Summit County's website has all application details....
events.location: Kimball Art Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Serve Park City: Park City's 3rd Annual 9/11 national Day of Service and Remembrance
events.description: Join Summit County for a barbed wire pull at the 910 Ranch! Enjoy a hands-on day of stewardship and community connection! We’ll work together to remove decommissioned barbed wire fencing, helping to restore wildlife movement and improve the health of the landscape. What to Bring & Wear: Wear long pants, long sleeves, and sturdy closed-toe shoes or boots. Pack sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen), water and snacks, and layers in case of changing weather. Please bring sunglasses to act as protective eyewear! Participants should bring thick, sturdy gloves for their protection and wire cutters if they have them. Summit County has a limited number of gloves and wire cutters to borrow. Safety Notice: Participation in barbed wire removal can be dangerous. Hazards may include cuts, punctures, scrapes, tripping, heavy lifting, sharp edges, and exposure to rusted metal or other environmental risks. By signing up and attending this activity, you acknowledge these risks, accept full responsibility for your participation, and agree to the terms of the waiver. A recent tetanus vaccination is recommended, though it is not required. Your safety is our priority—please work carefully, follow instructions, and look out for one another. This is a great opportunity to roll up your sleeves, meet like-minded people, and directly help enhance one of our community’s treasured open spaces....
events.location: Park City's City Park, 1400 Sullivan Road, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

What to Bring & Wear: Wear long pants, long sleeves, and sturdy closed-toe shoes or boots. Pack sun protection (hat, sunglasses, sunscreen), water and snacks, and layers in case of changing weather. Please bring sunglasses to act as protective eyewear! Participants should bring thick, sturdy gloves for their protection and wire cutters if they have them. Summit County has a limited number of gloves and wire cutters to borrow. Safety Notice: Participation in barbed wire removal can be dangerous. Hazards may include cuts, punctures, scrapes, tripping, heavy lifting, sharp edges, and exposure to rusted metal or other environmental risks. By signing up and attending this activity, you acknowledge these risks, accept full responsibility for your participation, and agree to the terms of the waiver. A recent tetanus vaccination is recommended, though it is not required. Your safety is our priority—please work carefully, follow instructions, and look out for one another. This is a great opportunity to roll up your sleeves, meet like-minded people, and directly help enhance one of our community’s treasured open spaces....
events.location: 910 Cattle Ranch, Park City, Utah

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....
events.location: Café Galleria, Midway, UT

events.title: ACE men's group
events.description: ACE men's group Thursday, October 15th | 5:30 PM | Alta Community Center | Free The ACE Men's Group purpose is to provide personal and professional support to men connected to the Alta and Little Cottonwood communities. The power in groups lies in the unique opportunity to give and receive multiple perspectives, support, encouragement and feedback to and from other individuals in a safe and confidential environment. The goal and hope is that you will benefit from your experience here. Daniel is a Licensed Clinical Social Worker practicing in Salt Lake City. His practice focuses on supporting individuals to develop insight into themselves, plan for making positive change and execution of those plans to reach their goals. Daniel is an lover of the outdoors, especially Alta and all it has to offer. Following meetings of each month: 6/18 7/16 8/20 9/17 10/15 ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all our attendees. Please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, October 22nd | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: Miles Glynn: Westernish
events.description: Join us October 23rd to kick off our first show of the fall, and experience new works by Miles Glynn at Gallery MAR, Park City. Miles Glynn grew up seeing the American West through a car window, the son of an Army photojournalist, trained from childhood to see the world in frames and compositions. That eye never left him. Working from Marfa, Texas, Glynn builds layered mixed-media paintings from vintage photographs, forgotten advertising, and Western ephemera, combining silkscreen, paint, and collage into works that feel at once deeply familiar and entirely new. The textures of deteriorating buildings, weathered billboards, and desert landscapes find their way into every surface, documents of time, resilience, and the stories that linger after everything else fades. His work doesn't ask you to see what he sees. It asks you to find what only you would....
events.location: Gallery MAR, 436 Main St, Park City, UT, 84060

events.title: salt lake ballet co-op with the lingo
events.description: salt lake ballet co-op with the lingo Sunday, August 9th | 5 PM Doors | 5:30 PM Ballet | Snowpine Lodge Grand Lawn | $10 | Tickets Required | Kids Under 5 are Free Salt Lake Ballet Cooperative, a professional-level movement cooperative, is a program of Deseret Experimental Opera Company \(DEXO\). Founded in 2013, DEXO has produced many premier works; incorporating both traditional and non-traditional approaches. By bringing together diverse specialists ranging from musicians to dancers to writers to actors, we strive for the age-old goal of opera: a work greater than the sum of its parts. This summer, Salt Lake Ballet Cooperative \(SLBC\) is thrilled to be partnering with local band, The Lingo. Lead vocalist Adam Borgoine, bassist Alex Millet, keyboardist Joe Pederson, guitarist Sam Morrison, and drummer Bridger Croxford make up this groovy psych-rock band. Their influence ranges from prototype punk to surf-rock from the 60s. Over the past several years, SLBC, a program of Deseret Experimental Opera, has cultivated partnerships with local musicians and bands to present original, unique performances. You are in for a truly one-of-a-kind experience with this all-new collaboration. One thing is for sure, this show promises to bring the groove! FACTS & TIPS: This event will take place on the Grand Lawn of the Snowpine Lodge You must bring your own blanket or LOW chairs \(must be 31.5" height or less\). Food and beverages available for purchase Outside food and beverages NOT allowed 5 PM Doors - 5:30pm Show begins ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility...
events.location: Snowpine Lodge Grand Lawn 10420 Little Cottonwood Canyon Road, Alta, UT

events.title: Live Music at Silver Lake
events.description: Food Trucks, Music & Scenic Adventures — Join us Friday through Sunday on Silver Lake Lawn for live music, local food trucks, and relaxed mountain afternoons. Settle into a lounge chair, enjoy a cold drink, and take in alpine views while a rotating lineup of artists fills the air with acoustic rock, country, blues, and more. With hiking trails just steps away, it’s an easy, all-day experience for everyone. BUY LIFT TICKETS THIS WEEKEND’S LINEUP Friday, August 7 Cry Wolf Saturday, August 8 Pompe n Honey Sunday, August 9 Vorraro
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.title: Chef Ryan Arensdorf Weekend
events.description: Fire & Flavor Festival — Experience Fire & Flavor Festival, where live-fire cooking meets alpine views. On August 8 and 9, Chef Ryan Arensdorf returns to take the lead with Kansas City-style BBQ, featuring slow smoked meats, classic sides, and sweet cornbread. Set on Silver Lake Lawn, this weekend brings bold, fire-driven flavor to a relaxed mountain setting, where guests can gather and refuel between activities. Throughout the lawn, discover standout culinary moments, from a showstopping eight-foot paella filled with fresh seafood and local meats to plank salmon, slow-cooked over the fire. Pair each bite with craft pours from Utah breweries and live music. Admission to the event is free with the purchase of a lift ticket . Food and beverage are available à la carte, with each culinary station offering a $25 plate.
events.location: Silver Lake Base Area, Deer Valley, Park City, UT

events.description: You've read the play - maybe seen the movie, the musical, e ven the ballet. But have you experienced the opera?August 21 at 7:00 PM & August 23 at 3:00 PM Eccles Center, Park City In Charles Gounod's Roméo et Juliette, Shakespeare's star-crossed lovers come to life amid the pageantry and passion of Verona, reimagined through the grand orchestral and theatrical language of French Romantic opera. Arrive early to explore displays of art, history, and themed concessions that set the scene for Roméo et Juliette, and stay after for a Q&A with the artists and creative team. events.title: Labor Day Experiences
events.description: Celebrate the close of summer with a long weekend designed for easygoing fun, outdoor play, and family-friendly moments. From lively daytime activities to relaxed evening experiences, Labor Day at Deer Valley offers the perfect setting to soak in the season’s final days. **DALY’S DINING & GAMES** ***Daly’s Pub & Rec*** **September 4 – 6 | 2:00pm – 9:00pm** Relax and recharge at Daly’s Pub & Rec, where casual dining meets interactive entertainment. Open throughout the afternoon and evening, Daly’s offers approachable fare, handcrafted beverages, bowling lanes, and arcade games, making it an easygoing destination to gather and enjoy the holiday weekend. **ANIMAL SHOW** ***Compass Sports*** **September 4 | 3:00pm** Meet fascinating animals native to Utah and hear engaging stories from their caretakers during this interactive wildlife presentation. Designed to educate and entertain, the animal show offers a memorable experience for guests of all ages. **CANDY LAND** ***5th Floor*** **September 4 – 6 | 3:00pm – 4:30pm** Children are invited to explore a curated candy station and select their favorite sweets to create a personalized assortment. A simple, playful activity designed especially for younger guests, Candy Land offers a cheerful way to enjoy a sweet moment during Labor Day weekend. **CHILDREN’S NIGHT OUT** ***Paintbox*** **September 4 & 5 | 5:00pm – 9:00pm** While parents enjoy an evening during Labor Day weekend, younger guests are invited to spend the night at Paintbox for a fun and thoughtfully curated experience. Children’s Night Out features engaging craft projects, a family-friendly movie, and a relaxed dinner, all guided by Montage Deer Valley’s trained team. Designed to feel playful, comfortable, and welcoming, the evening offers children a memorable night of creativity and connection while parents enjoy time of their own. *Reservations required.* **VEUVE CLICQUOT APRÈS LOUNGE: SUNSET SERIES** ***Après Lounge*** **September 4 & 5 ...

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: Fox US Open of Mountain Biking
events.description: The Official US National Mountain Bike Championship — The Fox US Open of Mountain Biking is coming to Deer Valley! Join us September 11–13, 2026, as Deer Valley hosts the final round of the Pro Downhill Series, the Official US National Mountain Bike Championship , on Bald Eagle Mountain. Over three days of competition, professional, amateur, youth, and adaptive athletes will compete for championship titles on Deer Valley's renowned downhill terrain. Pro Finals will take place on Saturday, September 12. Spectators can experience elite downhill racing, explore Deer Valley's lift-served bike park, and enjoy a weekend celebrating mountain biking at one of the sport's premier venues. Event access is free; however, a lift ticket is required to access race venues via chairlift.

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Gorgeous Gourds
events.description: Psychedelic Funk band Gorgeous Gourds combine smooth harmonies, deep tasty grooves, and original songwriting. The Gorgeous Gourd's just released album \(2/22/22\) "Peace and Love" reminds us why we go see and hear live music. The band has evolved to a rock solid synergistic group of accomplished musicians. Their powerful singing and songwriting is evident on their new CD, with 14 worthy cuts combining an eclectic combination of psychedelic funk, folk, tasty jam grooves, smooth harmonies and soulful ballads. Fred, Lucas, and Sarah all share the artful original songwriting. Iconic KRCL DJ John Florence commented on the new album, "Not a stinker in the bunch!” Formed in Salt Lake City, Utah in 2015, founding members are Fred Hayes \(bass, vocals\), and the strong and steady Bob Smith \(drums\). Lucas Jones \(lead guitar,vocals\) provides sublime and powerful guitar licks and vocals, together with the guitar of Jacob Dahlberg and the dazzling vocals of Heather McCartin. Whether you enjoy easing into a bluesy flow ride, or getting all the kinks out on the dance floor, the Gorgeous Gourds is the party you'll keep coming back to. The title of their new album "Peace and Love" is a message they spread with unbridled energy and enthusiasm. Like they say, "You're Gorgeous, we're the Gourds. Together we are Gorgeous Gourds." GorgeousGourds.net...

events.title: Old Town walking tour
events.description: Step beyond Main Street and experience Park City with a local! This guided tour blends mountain-town charm, historic buildings, Olympic legacy, art-filled tunnels, and more! Gain an insider perspective through a small-group setting designed to help you feel truly connected to Park City. We'll meet upstairs inside Old Town Transit Center (easily accessed by Park City's fare-free bus!) to wander for 1-1.5 hours and just under a mile. Weather-appropriate layers and a water bottle are recommended, families are encouraged, and gratuities are appreciated. Use code PR20 for 20% off!
events.location: Old Town Transit Center, Park City, UT

events.title: Pendry Plaza
events.description: Raised in a family full of musicians, music is a natural calling for Salt Lake City based artist Angie Petty. Taking influence from artists like Amy Winehouse, India Arie and Moonchild, Angie's unique sound sparkles in a pop/jazz world with a smoothness that will leave you wanting more. Packed with an arsenal full of tools, Petty is hands on when it comes to producing, arranging, writing, performing and recording her music. These many talents have lended opportunities in sharing stages with the likes of Grammy Award Winning artists Jon Batiste, Melvin Jones, Philip Lassiter and Elliot Yamin. In 2021, she released her debut album “Timing,” landing her song “Love Letter” on multiple Spotify editorial placements. Angie's mission is to share her life experiences through simple yet vulnerable lyrics to remind people they are never alone. A hidden gem in the making, Angie Petty is a rising star to watch, with multiple new releases and tour dates coming in 2024....
events.location: Pendry Park City, Park City, UT

events.title: Opera Around the World: Great Britain — Park City Opera
events.description: Paris is calling - and you don't need a plane ticket! Romantic, shimmering, and utterly enchanting, this evening features excerpts from PCO's upcoming performances of Roméo et Juliette https://www.parkcityopera.org/romeo-et-juliette - because nobody does passion quite like the French. Set against Park City's stunning mountain backdrop, we invite you to bring a picnic, gather your loved ones \(dogs included\), and enjoy opera and vocal chamber music in an open-air, relaxed community setting. This event is free and no tickets are required.

events.title: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town
events.description: secrets below alta: wild west archaeology in a mining boom town Thursday, January 14th | Our Lady of the Snows | 6PM | Free | Sign-up Required If you love Old West ghost-town history, you won't want to miss this! For the first time ever, the mining boomtown of Alta has been partially excavated, and what emerged from the ground is incredible. Thousands of artifacts were uncovered, including a fully intact bottle of 150-year-old alcohol, a leather hat, bullets, melted shot glasses, animal bones, and so much more. Discover what these objects reveal about the rowdy and genteel past of Little Cottonwood Canyon. Christopher Merritt received his Ph.D. from the University of Montana in Anthropology in 2010, focusing on archaeological and historical investigations of the Overseas Chinese. Before that, he received a Master's of Science in Industrial Archaeology from Michigan Technological University, where he studied the trade and consumption of Mormon-produced pottery in Utah. Since 2004, Merritt has worked in a variety of professional archaeological settings, including the United States Forest Service in Utah and Nevada, private archaeological contracting in Salt Lake City, and, finally, as the State Historic Preservation Officer for the Utah Division of State History. In addition to the full-time employment with the State of Utah, he is Adjunct Faculty in Anthropology at Salt Lake Community College and an instructor for the Osher Lifelong Learning Institute. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....
events.location: Our Lady of the Snows Center, Alta, UT
events.venue_name: Our Lady of the Snows Center

events.title: the art of arrangement
events.description: the art of arrangement Tuesday, August 25th | 6 PM | Alta Peruvian Lawn | $10 | Sign-up Required From Simple to Stunning! In this class, students will learn how to transform an ordinary store-bought bouquet into a stunning flower arrangement using floral design techniques. By applying principles such as balance, color harmony, proportion, and texture, simple flowers can be arranged into a creative and eye-catching display. Students will practice trimming stems, layering flowers, and adding greenery to create depth and movement, turning a basic bouquet into an elegant centerpiece. This event is for 10 and up. Monika was born and raised in Romania before moving to the United States to follow her passion for art and design. Over the years, she discovered a love for floral design and began creating beautiful arrangements that combine creativity and natural beauty. Four years ago, Monica moved to Utah, where she enjoys the stunning outdoor scenery and the welcoming people who make the state feel like home. ACE aims to create an inclusive and accessible environment for all of our attendees; please contact our Executive Director, Sara Gibbs, with any questions regarding accessibility....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description: Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date. A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere. Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: La Milpa - Harvest
events.description: **La Milpa Harvest** September 8, 2026 | 5:00 - 7:00 pm It is time to harvest! Together, we will gather as a community to harvest La Milpa and other culturally-relevant crops. We acknowledge ancestral and current Indigenous practices fas we recognize all that the land has provided. We hope for a bountiful harvest that allows people to bring some of the harvest home. This gathering will be in Spanish and English. A sample of delicious, traditional food will be offered. Registration is required for this free event. Doors open at 5 pm.; program begins at 5:30.

events.title: stamp carving
events.description: stamp carving Tuesday, September 15th | 6 PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn how to design, carve, and print with their own handmade rubber stamps. This class is especially good for people who want to learn the basics of stamp carving and printmaking. You will use carving tools, rubber carving blocks, inks, and printing materials during this class. At the end of the class, you will leave with a hand-carved stamp and the skills to continue creating your own custom designs at home. This event is for 9 and up. Melissa Mott is co-owner of Handcraft Revival Folk School & Market in Salt Lake City. She is passionate about preserving and sharing traditional handcraft skills and creating opportunities for people of all ages to learn through making. Over the years, she has taught a wide variety of classes, camps, and community programs focused on creativity, craftsmanship, and hands-on learning. Melissa believes that making things by hand helps us slow down, build confidence, and connect more deeply with both our work and one another. events.title: Military Appreciation Week
events.description: Join us at Snowbird for Military Appreciation Week. Active and retired Military and their immediate family will receive a FREE All-Day Summer Scenic Tram Ride pass to the top of Hidden Peak. How to Redeem Your Tickets: 1. Visit http://bit.ly/4n7RLQu, exclusively for Military members. Select the date and number of additional passes you would like to redeem. Add them to your Snowbird cart and checkout. 2. Assign your tickets and select whether each person needs a ticket card. 3. Fill out the Sheer ID verification with your information \(https://services.sheerid.com/verify/6407ade40bd23a3ee0613db1/\). 4. Once your military status is verified, input the code you receive into the box on the checkout page. 5. Finish the checkout process. Afterwards, you will receive a confirmation email with a QR code you can use to print your passes at one of the many Pick-Up Boxes located throughout the resort....

events.title: gesture drawing
events.description: gesture drawing Tuesday, October 13th | 6PM | Our Lady of the Snows | $10 | Sign-up Required In this class, students will learn to apply the principles of gesture drawing to still-life objects and the human figure. Gesture drawing is fast-paced and a great lesson in learning to be free. This event is for 10 and up. Cat is a local maker and small business owner in the Salt Lake Valley. Art has always been an essential part of Cat's life, through childhood and now. She had the pleasure of attending the University of Tennessee, Knoxville to earn her MS in Teacher Education and BFA in Printmaking and Drawing, with a minor in Art History. She was an art teacher for 8 years, stepping away in 2020 to pursue Honeysuckle Handmade Studio as her full-time job. events.title: Last Friday Gallery Stroll
events.description: Hosted by the Park City Gallery Association, the Last Friday Gallery Stroll, is a fun \(and free\) monthly community event dedicated to highlighting artists and special exhibits at art galleries throughout town. Park City's art scene continues to thrive, offering a robust variety of unique art collections from local, national, and international artists. On the last Friday of each month, seventeen of our art galleries open their doors for visitors and locals alike to enjoy light refreshments and to admire their latest art installations. Park City Gallery Stroll runs from 6:00 - 9:00pm on the last Friday of each month. It's the perfect way to explore the Park City art scene while enjoying scenic Historic Main Street and our beautiful alpine evenings....
events.location: Main Street, Park City, UT
